name: Large Language Models to Identify Social Determinants of Health to Enhance Healthcare Services
description: This study is developing an AI to ask patients about social determinants of health domains before their healthcare visit. We are asking patients about healthcare navigation, substance use, transportation, health literacy, mood and mental health, financial challenges, food security, stress management, and employment. We are also asking providers to give input about how the program would be the most valuable and practical.
preface:
You are being asked to be in this research study because you are a provider at the University of Colorado Healthcare System.

If you join the study, you will be asked a few questions and asked for your feedback about an AI tool for providers. This study is designed to learn more about patient social determinants of health that may be unintentionally missed or uneasy to share with a doctor or care team.

Possible risks include a breach of confidentiality or privacy but every effort will be made to secure the data adhering to current institutional policies and HIPAA regulations.

We will protect your privacy and confidentiality by de-identifying your answers and storing them on a password-protected University of Colorado secure server.

Your choice to participate in the study is voluntary and your decision will not affect your employment or any current or future ability to participate in such studies.

The data we collect will be used for this study but may also be important for future research. Your data may be used for future research or distributed to other researchers for future study without additional consent if information that identifies you is removed from the data.

If you have questions, you can contact the PI Foster Goss, DO, MMSc at any time.
Associate Professor, Department of Emergency Medicine, School of Medicine
Leprino Building, 12401 East 17th Ave, Aurora, CO 80045
Telephone: 909-878-6777
Email: foster.goss@ucdenver.edu

You may have questions about your rights as someone in this study. If you have questions, you can call COMIRB (the responsible Institutional Review Board). Their number is (303) 724-1055 and their email is comirb@ucdenver.edu.

By completing the focus group feedback, you are agreeing to participate in this research
study.
